pi_items.source: Task description requests IT department employees with department_id = 60. The export_result.sh script also uses this in its ground truth query.
Task description requests employees with a salary greater than 5000. The export_result.sh script also uses this in its ground truth query.
Task description explicitly lists these columns to be returned. The export_result.sh script validates these exact columns in its ground truth query.

Inspection of data/hr_schema.sql reveals exactly 2 IT employees (department 60) with a salary > 5000 (Alexander Hunold with 9000, and Bruce Ernst with 6000). The setup_task.sh script also expects at least 2 rows.
Task description explicitly requires results to be exported to this file path. Both setup_task.sh and export_result.sh use this path.
Task description and scripts/setup_oracle.sh which creates the HR user.
Task description and scripts/setup_oracle.sh which sets the HR user's password.
Task description and scripts/setup_oracle.sh which starts the container on port 1521 using the XEPDB1 pluggable database.

privileged_info_summary: The Oracle HR sample schema database contains exactly two IT department employees (department_id 60) who meet the salary > 5000 requirement (Alexander Hunold and Bruce Ernst). The expected database connection credentials are hr/hr123 at localhost:1521/XEPDB1, and the target output file is /tmp/query_results.txt, containing the employee_id, first_name, last_name, salary, and job_id columns.
